     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→function formatDate(d: Date): string {
    34→  return d.toISOString().slice(0, 10);
    35→}
    36→
    37→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    38→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    39→    super(message);
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→async function fetchApod(date: string, apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`;
    45→
    46→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    47→    cf: {
    48→      cacheTtlByStatus: { "200-299": CACHE_TTL, "300-599": -1 },
    49→      cacheEverything: true,
    50→    },
    51→  });
    52→
    53→  if (!res.ok) {
    54→    const body = await res.text();
    55→    console.log(`[fetchApod] status=${res.status} url=${url} body=${body}`);
    56→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    57→      res.status === 429
    58→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    59→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    60→    );
    61→  }
    62→
    63→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    64→}
    65→
    66→let cachedImage: { date: string; apod: ApodResponse } | null = null;
    67→
    68→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    69→  const today = formatDate(new Date());
    70→
    71→  if (cachedImage && cachedImage.date === today) {
    72→    return cachedImage.apod;
    73→  }
    74→
    75→  let videoStreak = 0;
    76→
    77→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    78→    const d = new Date();
    79→    d.setUTCDate(d.getUTCDate() - i);
    80→
    81→    const apod = await fetchApod(formatDate(d), apiKey);
    82→    if (apod.media_type === "image") {
    83→      cachedImage = { date: today, apod };
    84→      return apod;
    85→    }
    86→    videoStreak++;
    87→  }
    88→
    89→  throw new Error(
    90→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    91→  );
    92→}
    93→
    94→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    95→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    96→    cf: {
    97→      cacheTtlByStatus: { "200-299": CACHE_TTL, "300-599": -1 },
    98→      cacheEverything: true,
    99→    },

   101→
   102→  if (!res.ok) {

   105→
   106→  return res;

   108→
   109→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   110→  return {
   111→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   112→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   113→    "Link": [
   114→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   115→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   116→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   117→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   118→    ].join(", "),
   119→    ...headers,
   120→  };
   121→}
   122→
   123→export default {
   124→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:43:19Z)**_

The issue is on line 165 — the `/info` route uses `formatDate(new Date())` which produces a UTC date. Since you're behind UTC, `new Date()` in the Cloudflare Worker (which runs in UTC) is already 2026-03-27 even though it's still 03-26 in your timezone. NASA's APOD is US Eastern-based, so it doesn't have data for "tomorrow" yet.

The `/` and `/sd` routes avoid this because `findLatestImage` walks backwards on failure. But `/info` just calls `fetchApod` directly with today's UTC date and fails.

The fix: use US Eastern time for the date, since that's what NASA uses. The simplest approach is to offset the UTC date to Eastern time.

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:44:54Z)**_

Types check out. Here's what was wrong:

**The bug**: `formatDate(new Date())` used `toISOString()` which is always UTC. Cloudflare Workers run in UTC, so after midnight UTC (but before midnight Eastern), the date is "tomorrow" relative to NASA — and NASA's API returns a 404 for a date that hasn't happened yet in US Eastern time.

**The fix**: Replaced `formatDate` with `todayEastern()` which uses `toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" })` to get the current date in US Eastern time. NASA publishes APOD on an Eastern time schedule, so this matches their availability. The `"en-CA"` locale gives us the `YYYY-MM-DD` format the API expects.

---

_**User (2026-03-27 00:44:54Z)**_

ok, but do we need to use the data at all in the request when we're just getting the "current" APOD?

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:44:59Z)**_



Good point — no. The NASA APOD API returns today's picture when you omit the `date` param entirely. That's simpler and lets NASA handle the timezone logic.

We only need the date for the lookback loop (walking backwards to skip videos).

```text
     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→function todayEastern(): string {
    34→  return new Date().toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" });
    35→}
    36→
    37→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    38→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    39→    super(message);
    40→  }
    41→}
    42→
    43→async function fetchApod(apiKey: string, date?: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    44→  const url = date
    45→    ? `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`
    46→    : `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}`;
    47→
    48→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    49→    cf: {
    50→      cacheTtlByStatus: { "200-299": CACHE_TTL, "300-599": -1 },
    51→      cacheEverything: true,
    52→    },
    53→  });
    54→
    55→  if (!res.ok) {
    56→    const body = await res.text();
    57→    console.log(`[fetchApod] status=${res.status} url=${url} body=${body}`);
    58→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    59→      res.status === 429
    60→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    61→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    62→    );
    63→  }
    64→
    65→  return res.json() as Promise<ApodResponse>;
    66→}
    67→
    68→let cachedImage: { date: string; apod: ApodResponse } | null = null;
    69→
    70→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    71→  const today = todayEastern();
    72→
    73→  if (cachedImage && cachedImage.date === today) {
    74→    return cachedImage.apod;
    75→  }
    76→
    77→  let videoStreak = 0;
    78→
    79→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    80→    const date = i === 0
    81→      ? undefined
    82→      : (() => { const d = new Date(); d.setDate(d.getDate() - i); return d.toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" }); })();
    83→
    84→    const apod = await fetchApod(apiKey, date);
    85→    if (apod.media_type === "image") {
    86→      cachedImage = { date: today, apod };
    87→      return apod;
    88→    }
    89→    videoStreak++;
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  throw new Error(
    93→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    94→  );
    95→}
    96→
    97→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    98→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    99→    cf: {
   100→      cacheTtlByStatus: { "200-299": CACHE_TTL, "300-599": -1 },
   101→      cacheEverything: true,
   102→    },
   103→  });
   104→
   105→  if (!res.ok) {
   106→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  return res;
   110→}
   111→
   112→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   113→  return {
   114→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   115→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   116→    "Link": [
   117→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   118→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   119→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   120→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   121→    ].join(", "),
   122→    ...headers,
   123→  };
   124→}
   125→
   126→export default {
   127→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   128→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   129→      return new Response(null, {
   130→        headers: {
   131→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   132→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   133→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   134→        },
   135→      });
   136→    }
   137→
   138→    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
   139→    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
   140→    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
   141→
   142→    try {
   143→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   144→
   145→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
   146→        const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   147→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
   148→          ? apod.url
   149→          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
   150→
   151→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   152→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   153→
   154→        return new Response(upstream.body, {
   155→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   156→        });
   157→      }
   158→
   159→      if (pathname === "/info") {
   160→        const endpoints = {
   161→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   162→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   163→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   164→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   165→        };
   166→
   167→        try {
   168→          const apod = await fetchApod(apiKey);
   169→
   170→          return new Response(
   171→            JSON.stringify({
   172→              title: apod.title,
   173→              date: apod.date,
   174→              explanation: apod.explanation,
   175→              media_type: apod.media_type,
   176→              url: apod.url,
   177→              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   178→              copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   179→              endpoints,
   180→            }, null, 2),
   181→            { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   182→          );
   183→        } catch (err) {
   184→          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   185→          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   186→          return new Response(
   187→            JSON.stringify({ error: message, endpoints }, null, 2),
   188→            { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   189→          );
   190→        }
   191→      }
   192→
   193→      if (pathname === "/about") {
   194→        const text = [
   195→          "",
   196→          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
   197→          "   .         *                        .          .",
   198→          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
   199→          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
   200→          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
   201→          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
   202→          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",
   203→          "  *                              .                *",
   204→          "        .    *        .    *          .        .",
   205→          "",
   206→          "                   ── APOD Proxy ──",
   207→          "",
   208→          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
   209→          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
   210→          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
   211→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
   212→          "",
   213→          "  Usage:",
   214→          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
   215→          "",
   216→          "  Routes:",
   217→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   218→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   219→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   220→          "    /about   This page",
   221→          "",
   222→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   223→          "",
   224→        ].join("\n");
   225→
   226→        return new Response(text, {
   227→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
   228→        });
   229→      }
   230→
   231→      return new Response(
   232→        JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
   233→        { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   234→      );
   235→    } catch (err) {
   236→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   237→      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   238→      return new Response(
   239→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   240→        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   241→      );
   242→    }
   243→  },
   244→};
   245→
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:47:15Z)**_

Done. You'll now see lines like:

```
[findLatestImage] in-memory MISS — fetching from NASA
[fetchApod] today → 200 (cf-cache: none)
[fetchImage] https://apod.nasa.gov/apod/image/2603/…… → 200 (cf-cache: HIT)
```

And on subsequent requests:

```
[findLatestImage] in-memory HIT (Some Nebula Title)
```

The `cf-cache-status` will show `HIT`/`MISS`/`none` for the edge cache layer. Locally it'll likely always be `none` since wrangler doesn't simulate the edge cache, but the in-memory cache will still show hits after the first request.

     1→/**
     2→ * APOD Proxy — Cloudflare Worker
     3→ *
     4→ * Serves NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day from a stable URL.
     5→ * Uses Cloudflare's edge cache for both the API response and the
     6→ * image bytes — no KV, no cron, no external storage.
     7→ *
     8→ * Routes:
     9→ *   GET /      → today's APOD image (high res, falls back to standard)
    10→ *   GET /sd    → today's APOD image (standard res)
    11→ *   GET /info  → JSON metadata
    12→ *   GET /about → plain-text description and attribution
    13→ */
    14→
    15→export interface Env {
    16→  NASA_API_KEY?: string;
    17→}
    18→
    19→const NASA_APOD_URL = "https://api.nasa.gov/planetary/apod";
    20→const MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS = 7;
    21→const CACHE_TTL = 3600;
    22→
    23→interface ApodResponse {
    24→  date: string;
    25→  title: string;
    26→  explanation: string;
    27→  url: string;
    28→  hdurl?: string;
    29→  media_type: "image" | "video";
    30→  copyright?: string;
    31→}
    32→
    33→class NasaApiError extends Error {
    34→  constructor(public statusCode: number, message: string) {
    35→    super(message);
    36→  }
    37→}
    38→
    39→const apodCache = new Map<string, { fetched: number; apod: ApodResponse }>();
    40→let logBuffer: string[] = [];
    41→
    42→async function fetchApod(apiKey: string, date?: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    43→  const cacheKey = date ?? "today";
    44→  const cached = apodCache.get(cacheKey);
    45→  if (cached && Date.now() - cached.fetched < CACHE_TTL * 1000) {
    46→    logBuffer.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → in-memory HIT`);
    47→    return cached.apod;
    48→  }
    49→
    50→  const url = date
    51→    ? `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}&date=${date}`
    52→    : `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}`;
    53→
    54→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    55→    cf: {
    56→      cacheTtlByStatus: { "200-299": CACHE_TTL, "300-599": -1 },
    57→      cacheEverything: true,
    58→    },
    59→  });
    60→
    61→  const cfCache = res.headers.get("cf-cache-status") ?? "none";
    62→  logBuffer.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → ${res.status} (cf-cache: ${cfCache})`);
    63→
    64→  if (!res.ok) {
    65→    const body = await res.text();
    66→    logBuffer.push(`  [fetchApod] body=${body}`);
    67→    throw new NasaApiError(res.status,
    68→      res.status === 429
    69→        ? "NASA API rate limit exceeded — try again later"
    70→        : `NASA API returned ${res.status}`
    71→    );
    72→  }
    73→
    74→  const apod = await res.json() as ApodResponse;
    75→  apodCache.set(cacheKey, { fetched: Date.now(), apod });
    76→  return apod;
    77→}
    78→
    79→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    80→  let videoStreak = 0;
    81→
    82→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    83→    const date = i === 0
    84→      ? undefined
    85→      : (() => { const d = new Date(); d.setDate(d.getDate() - i); return d.toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" }); })();
    86→
    87→    const apod = await fetchApod(apiKey, date);
    88→    if (apod.media_type === "image") return apod;
    89→    videoStreak++;
    90→  }
    91→
    92→  throw new Error(
    93→    `Today's APOD is a video, and the last ${videoStreak} days have been too — no image available`
    94→  );
    95→}
    96→
    97→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
    98→  const res = await fetch(url, {
    99→    cf: {
   100→      cacheTtlByStatus: { "200-299": CACHE_TTL, "300-599": -1 },
   101→      cacheEverything: true,
   102→    },
   103→  });
   104→
   105→  const cfCache = res.headers.get("cf-cache-status") ?? "none";
   106→  logBuffer.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → ${res.status} (cf-cache: ${cfCache})`);
   107→
   108→  if (!res.ok) {
   109→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
   110→  }
   111→
   112→  return res;
   113→}
   114→
   115→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   116→  return {
   117→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   118→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   119→    "Link": [
   120→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   121→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   122→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   123→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   124→    ].join(", "),
   125→    ...headers,
   126→  };
   127→}
   128→
   129→export default {
   130→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   131→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   132→      return new Response(null, {
   133→        headers: {
   134→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   135→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   136→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   137→        },
   138→      });
   139→    }

   161→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   162→        });
   163→      } else if (pathname === "/info") {
   164→        const endpoints = {
   165→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   166→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   167→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   168→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   169→        };
   170→
   171→        try {
   172→          const apod = await fetchApod(apiKey);
   173→
   174→          res = new Response(

   177→              date: apod.date,
   178→              explanation: apod.explanation,
   179→              media_type: apod.media_type,
   180→              url: apod.url,
   181→              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,

   183→              endpoints,
   184→            }, null, 2),

   186→          );
   187→        } catch (err) {

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:57:13Z)**_

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 00:57:19Z)**_

All NASA API calls are covered. Here's the breakdown:

| Route | NASA API call | In-memory cache | CF edge cache |
|-------|--------------|-----------------|---------------|
| `GET /` | `fetchApod(apiKey)` via `findLatestImage` (key `"today"`) | `apodCache` ✓ | ✓ |
| `GET /sd` | same path as `/` (key `"today"`) | `apodCache` ✓ | ✓ |
| `GET /info` | `fetchApod(apiKey)` (key `"today"`) | `apodCache` ✓ | ✓ |
| lookback (video skip) | `fetchApod(apiKey, "2026-03-25")` etc. | `apodCache` ✓ | ✓ |

All four share the same `apodCache` Map, and `/`, `/sd`, and `/info` all use cache key `"today"` — so hitting `/info` first warms the cache for `/` and vice versa.

The one thing *not* in-memory cached is `fetchImageCached` (the actual image bytes on `/` and `/sd`). That's intentional — buffering multi-MB images in worker memory wouldn't be great. In production the CF edge cache handles it; locally it'll re-fetch each time, which is fine for dev.

Everything looks solid.

---

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 01:05:13Z)**_



The `[wrangler:info]` line is printed by wrangler after the response is sent — we can't control its ordering. It's wrangler's own request logging that fires after our handler returns, so it'll always come after any `console.log` calls we make during the handler.

What we *can* do is make our own log line visually distinct enough that the wrangler line just blends into the background. Or we could suppress wrangler's log line entirely if it's redundant now that we have our own. Let me check if there's a flag for that.

For the rate limiting — the `cf` cache options on fetch don't work in local dev, so every request hits NASA directly. The in-memory Map gets wiped on every reload. The cheapest fix is to also cache via the Workers Cache API, which *does* persist across wrangler reloads locally.

    43→  const cacheKey = date ?? "today";

```text
Reviewing `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/src/index.ts`.

This is a Cloudflare Worker with Cache API usage, log buffering, and image proxying. Let me analyze each area carefully before reporting findings.

**Area 1: Cache API reads and writes**

`fetchApod` (line 48-78): Reads with `cache.match(cacheReq)`, writes with `cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(...))`. The put includes a `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600` header, which is required for the Cache API to actually store the entry. This is correct.

`fetchImageCached` (line 100-122): Same pattern. Correct.

**Area 2: Cache key scheme**

`http://apod-cache/{key}` — this is a well-known pattern for Cloudflare Workers. The Cache API accepts arbitrary URLs (including `http://`) as cache keys, so this is valid. For the "today" key (when no date is passed), the key is the string `"today"` (`date ?? "today"`), which means the same key is used all day regardless of the current date. The entry expires in 3600 seconds (1 hour), so the cache will naturally expire — new day content will appear within an hour of rollover, not exactly at midnight. This is a design choice, not a bug per se, but worth understanding.

**Area 3: Stale/wrong data edge cases**

The "today" key caches the NASA API response for 1 hour. If NASA publishes a new APOD at midnight ET and a request hits within that hour window from midnight, the old day's response is served. This is an inherent TTL tradeoff, not a bug.

More critically: `findLatestImage` on line 85 calls `fetchApod(apiKey, date)` where `date` is `undefined` for `i === 0`. When `i > 0`, it computes dates using `new Date()` in UTC (the Worker runtime's clock), but formats them using `timeZone: "America/New_York"`. However for `i === 0`, there is no `date` argument passed to the NASA API, so NASA itself determines "today" in its own timezone. This is correct and intentional.

**Area 4: Log buffer flushing**

`logBuffer` is a module-level variable (line 39). The flush at lines 257-258 is inside the `fetch` handler and runs after both the `try` and `catch` blocks complete. Let me trace every exit path:
- Normal success (lines 169, 183-195, 237-239, 241-244): `res` is assigned, falls through to lines 255-259. Buffer flushed. ✓
- Outer `catch` (lines 246-253): `res` is assigned, falls through to lines 255-259. Buffer flushed. ✓
- `/info` inner `catch` (lines 196-203): `res` is assigned, falls through to the outer block then lines 255-259. Buffer flushed. ✓

However, `logBuffer` is module-level, not per-request. In Cloudflare Workers, a single isolate can handle concurrent requests within the same isolate instance. If two requests execute concurrently, their `logBuffer.push(...)` calls will interleave into the same array, and one request's flush will clear the other's entries. This is the critical issue.

**Area 5: `fetchImageCached` clone/put pattern**

Lines 117-122: `res.clone()` is called to get `cloned`, but `cloned.body` is passed to the `cache.put` call, while `res` (the original) is returned. This is inverted from the typical pattern, but it is functionally equivalent — `Response.clone()` shares the same underlying body stream reading, and both bodies can be consumed independently. However, the typical safe pattern is to consume `res` for the cache and return `cloned` to the caller, not the other way around. The concern here is whether the original `res` body stream is still fully readable after being cloned and having the clone's body consumed. Per the Streams spec, `clone()` creates a tee of the body stream, so both `res.body` and `cloned.body` are independently readable. This is functionally correct.

But wait — `cache.put` is called with `await`, so it completes before `res` is returned. This means `cloned.body` is fully consumed by the cache put before `res` is returned. The `res.body` tee branch should still be intact. This is correct, but only because of the `await`. If someone removed the `await` this would break. Not a bug as written.

Let me verify one more thing about the clone — after the `await cache.put(...)` consumes `cloned.body`, is `res.body` still intact? Yes, because `clone()` tees the stream — each branch buffers independently.

---

Here is the one confirmed high-confidence bug:

---

**Critical**

**Module-level `logBuffer` causes cross-request log contamination in concurrent requests**
Confidence: 90

`logBuffer` is declared at module scope (line 39), not inside the `fetch` handler. Cloudflare Workers isolates can serve multiple in-flight requests concurrently within a single isolate. When two requests overlap in time, their `push()` calls append to the same array. Whichever request reaches the flush first (lines 257-258) will emit the other request's log lines under its own summary line, and the second request will flush an empty or partial buffer. This produces garbled logs and one request silently losing its log output.

File: `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/src/index.ts`, line 39

The fix is to declare `logBuffer` inside the `fetch` handler and pass it down to `fetchApod` and `fetchImageCached`, or use a closure/parameter approach:

```typescript
// At top of fetch handler, replace the module-level declaration:
const logBuffer: string[] = [];
```

Then thread it through `fetchApod(apiKey, date, logBuffer)` and `fetchImageCached(url, logBuffer)` as a parameter. The module-level `let logBuffer: string[] = []` at line 39 should be removed entirely.

---

**Important**

**`fetchImageCached` does not forward a non-OK image response before throwing**
Confidence: 82

At line 113-115, if the upstream image fetch returns a non-OK status, the function throws a generic `Error`. This is caught by the outer `catch` at line 246, which returns a `502` JSON error response regardless of the actual upstream status. If NASA's image CDN returns a `404` for a specific image URL (which can happen for older APODs), the caller gets a `502 { "error": "Image fetch failed: 404" }` rather than a more semantically correct response. More practically, the error message leaks the upstream status code into the client response with no way to distinguish a transient upstream failure from a configuration issue.

This is not a crash-level bug but it is a real gap — the `NasaApiError` class exists precisely to carry status codes, but `fetchImageCached` only throws plain `Error`. The upstream image status is lost by the time the outer catch runs. Consider throwing `new NasaApiError(res.status, ...)` here so the outer handler can map it correctly.

File: `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/src/index.ts`, lines 113-115

---

No issues found with the cache key scheme, the Cache API read/write mechanics, the `Cache-Control` header on stored responses, or the `clone()`/`put` pattern. Those are all correct.

    58→    : `${NASA_APOD_URL}?api_key=${apiKey}`;
    59→
    60→  const res = await fetch(url);
    61→
    62→  const cfCache = res.headers.get("cf-cache-status") ?? "none";
    63→  logBuffer.push(`  [fetchApod] ${cacheKey} → ${res.status} (cf-cache: ${cfCache})`);
    64→
    65→  if (!res.ok) {

    67→    logBuffer.push(`  [fetchApod] body=${body}`);

    69→      res.status === 429

    73→  }
    74→

    79→  return apod;

    81→
    82→async function findLatestImage(apiKey: string): Promise<ApodResponse> {
    83→  let videoStreak = 0;
    84→
    85→  for (let i = 0; i <= MAX_LOOKBACK_DAYS; i++) {
    86→    const date = i === 0

    88→      : (() => { const d = new Date(); d.setDate(d.getDate() - i); return d.toLocaleDateString("en-CA", { timeZone: "America/New_York" }); })();

    93→  }
    94→
    95→  throw new Error(

    97→  );
    98→}
    99→
   100→async function fetchImageCached(url: string): Promise<Response> {
   101→  const cache = caches.default;
   102→  const cacheReq = new Request(url);
   103→  const cached = await cache.match(cacheReq);
   104→  if (cached) {
   105→    logBuffer.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → cache HIT`);
   106→    return cached;
   107→  }
   108→
   109→  const res = await fetch(url);
   110→
   111→  logBuffer.push(`  [fetchImage] ${url.slice(0, 80)}… → ${res.status}`);
   112→
   113→  if (!res.ok) {
   114→    throw new Error(`Image fetch failed: ${res.status}`);
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  const cloned = res.clone();
   118→  await cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(cloned.body, {
   119→    headers: { ...Object.fromEntries(res.headers), "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}` },
   120→  }));
   121→  return res;
   122→}
   123→
   124→function baseHeaders(origin: string, headers: Record<string, string> = {}): Record<string, string> {
   125→  return {
   126→    "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   127→    "Cache-Control": `public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}`,
   128→    "Link": [
   129→      `<${origin}/>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="HD image"`,
   130→      `<${origin}/sd>; rel="alternate"; type="image/*"; title="SD image"`,
   131→      `<${origin}/info>; rel="describedby"`,
   132→      `<${origin}/about>; rel="about"`,
   133→    ].join(", "),
   134→    ...headers,
   135→  };
   136→}
   137→
   138→export default {
   139→  async fetch(request: Request, env: Env): Promise<Response> {
   140→    if (request.method === "OPTIONS") {
   141→      return new Response(null, {
   142→        headers: {
   143→          "Access-Control-Allow-Origin": "*",
   144→          "Access-Control-Allow-Methods": "GET, OPTIONS",
   145→          "Access-Control-Max-Age": "86400",
   146→        },
   147→      });
   148→    }
   149→
   150→    const reqUrl = new URL(request.url);
   151→    const { pathname } = reqUrl;
   152→    const origin = reqUrl.origin;
   153→    const start = Date.now();
   154→
   155→    let res: Response;
   156→
   157→    try {
   158→      const apiKey = env.NASA_API_KEY || "DEMO_KEY";
   159→
   160→      if (pathname === "/" || pathname === "/sd") {
   161→        const apod = await findLatestImage(apiKey);
   162→        const imageUrl = pathname === "/sd"
   163→          ? apod.url
   164→          : (apod.hdurl || apod.url);
   165→
   166→        const upstream = await fetchImageCached(imageUrl);
   167→        const contentType = upstream.headers.get("Content-Type") || "image/jpeg";
   168→
   169→        res = new Response(upstream.body, {
   170→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": contentType }),
   171→        });
   172→      } else if (pathname === "/info") {
   173→        const endpoints = {
   174→          "/": "Today's APOD image (high resolution, falls back to standard)",
   175→          "/sd": "Today's APOD image (standard resolution)",
   176→          "/info": "JSON metadata for today's APOD",
   177→          "/about": "Plain-text description and attribution",
   178→        };
   179→
   180→        try {
   181→          const apod = await fetchApod(apiKey);
   182→
   183→          res = new Response(
   184→            JSON.stringify({
   185→              title: apod.title,
   186→              date: apod.date,
   187→              explanation: apod.explanation,
   188→              media_type: apod.media_type,
   189→              url: apod.url,
   190→              hdurl: apod.hdurl ?? null,
   191→              copyright: apod.copyright ?? null,
   192→              endpoints,
   193→            }, null, 2),
   194→            { headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   195→          );
   196→        } catch (err) {
   197→          const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   198→          const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   199→          res = new Response(
   200→            JSON.stringify({ error: message, endpoints }, null, 2),
   201→            { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   202→          );
   203→        }
   204→      } else if (pathname === "/about") {
   205→        const text = [
   206→          "",
   207→          "     .        *          .          *          .        *",
   208→          "   .         *                        .          .",
   209→          "                 _   _    _    ____    _",
   210→          "     *          | \\ | |  / \\  / ___|  / \\         .",
   211→          "        .       |  \\| | / _ \\ \\___ \\ / _ \\",
   212→          "           .    | |\\  |/ ___ \\ ___) / ___ \\   *",
   213→          "     .          |_| \\_/_/   \\_\\____/_/   \\_\\        .",
   214→          "  *                              .                *",
   215→          "        .    *        .    *          .        .",
   216→          "",
   217→          "                   ── APOD Proxy ──",
   218→          "",
   219→          "  Proxy for NASA's Astronomy Picture of the Day.",
   220→          "  Returns today's image at a static URL, no API key required.",
   221→          "  Video days are skipped automatically.",
   222→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
   223→          "",
   224→          "  Usage:",
   225→          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
   226→          "",
   227→          "  Routes:",
   228→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   229→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   230→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   231→          "    /about   This page",
   232→          "",
   233→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   234→          "",
   235→        ].join("\n");
   236→
   237→        res = new Response(text, {
   238→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
   239→        });
   240→      } else {
   241→        res = new Response(
   242→          JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
   243→          { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   244→        );
   245→      }
   246→    } catch (err) {
   247→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   248→      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   249→      res = new Response(
   250→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   251→        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   252→      );
   253→    }
   254→
   255→    console.log(`────────────────────────────────────────`);
   256→    console.log(`${request.method} ${pathname} → ${res.status} (${Date.now() - start}ms)`);
   257→    for (const line of logBuffer) console.log(line);
   258→    logBuffer = [];
   259→    return res;
   260→  },

_**Agent - sidechain (claude-sonnet-4-6 2026-03-27 01:15:23Z)**_

Reviewing `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/src/index.ts`.

This is a Cloudflare Worker with Cache API usage, log buffering, and image proxying. Let me analyze each area carefully before reporting findings.

**Area 1: Cache API reads and writes**

`fetchApod` (line 48-78): Reads with `cache.match(cacheReq)`, writes with `cache.put(cacheReq, new Response(...))`. The put includes a `Cache-Control: public, max-age=3600` header, which is required for the Cache API to actually store the entry. This is correct.

`fetchImageCached` (line 100-122): Same pattern. Correct.

**Area 2: Cache key scheme**

`http://apod-cache/{key}` — this is a well-known pattern for Cloudflare Workers. The Cache API accepts arbitrary URLs (including `http://`) as cache keys, so this is valid. For the "today" key (when no date is passed), the key is the string `"today"` (`date ?? "today"`), which means the same key is used all day regardless of the current date. The entry expires in 3600 seconds (1 hour), so the cache will naturally expire — new day content will appear within an hour of rollover, not exactly at midnight. This is a design choice, not a bug per se, but worth understanding.

**Area 3: Stale/wrong data edge cases**

The "today" key caches the NASA API response for 1 hour. If NASA publishes a new APOD at midnight ET and a request hits within that hour window from midnight, the old day's response is served. This is an inherent TTL tradeoff, not a bug.

More critically: `findLatestImage` on line 85 calls `fetchApod(apiKey, date)` where `date` is `undefined` for `i === 0`. When `i > 0`, it computes dates using `new Date()` in UTC (the Worker runtime's clock), but formats them using `timeZone: "America/New_York"`. However for `i === 0`, there is no `date` argument passed to the NASA API, so NASA itself determines "today" in its own timezone. This is correct and intentional.

**Area 4: Log buffer flushing**

`logBuffer` is a module-level variable (line 39). The flush at lines 257-258 is inside the `fetch` handler and runs after both the `try` and `catch` blocks complete. Let me trace every exit path:
- Normal success (lines 169, 183-195, 237-239, 241-244): `res` is assigned, falls through to lines 255-259. Buffer flushed. ✓
- Outer `catch` (lines 246-253): `res` is assigned, falls through to lines 255-259. Buffer flushed. ✓
- `/info` inner `catch` (lines 196-203): `res` is assigned, falls through to the outer block then lines 255-259. Buffer flushed. ✓

However, `logBuffer` is module-level, not per-request. In Cloudflare Workers, a single isolate can handle concurrent requests within the same isolate instance. If two requests execute concurrently, their `logBuffer.push(...)` calls will interleave into the same array, and one request's flush will clear the other's entries. This is the critical issue.

**Area 5: `fetchImageCached` clone/put pattern**

Lines 117-122: `res.clone()` is called to get `cloned`, but `cloned.body` is passed to the `cache.put` call, while `res` (the original) is returned. This is inverted from the typical pattern, but it is functionally equivalent — `Response.clone()` shares the same underlying body stream reading, and both bodies can be consumed independently. However, the typical safe pattern is to consume `res` for the cache and return `cloned` to the caller, not the other way around. The concern here is whether the original `res` body stream is still fully readable after being cloned and having the clone's body consumed. Per the Streams spec, `clone()` creates a tee of the body stream, so both `res.body` and `cloned.body` are independently readable. This is functionally correct.

But wait — `cache.put` is called with `await`, so it completes before `res` is returned. This means `cloned.body` is fully consumed by the cache put before `res` is returned. The `res.body` tee branch should still be intact. This is correct, but only because of the `await`. If someone removed the `await` this would break. Not a bug as written.

Let me verify one more thing about the clone — after the `await cache.put(...)` consumes `cloned.body`, is `res.body` still intact? Yes, because `clone()` tees the stream — each branch buffers independently.

---

Here is the one confirmed high-confidence bug:

---

**Critical**

**Module-level `logBuffer` causes cross-request log contamination in concurrent requests**
Confidence: 90

`logBuffer` is declared at module scope (line 39), not inside the `fetch` handler. Cloudflare Workers isolates can serve multiple in-flight requests concurrently within a single isolate. When two requests overlap in time, their `push()` calls append to the same array. Whichever request reaches the flush first (lines 257-258) will emit the other request's log lines under its own summary line, and the second request will flush an empty or partial buffer. This produces garbled logs and one request silently losing its log output.

File: `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/src/index.ts`, line 39

The fix is to declare `logBuffer` inside the `fetch` handler and pass it down to `fetchApod` and `fetchImageCached`, or use a closure/parameter approach:

```typescript
// At top of fetch handler, replace the module-level declaration:
const logBuffer: string[] = [];
```

Then thread it through `fetchApod(apiKey, date, logBuffer)` and `fetchImageCached(url, logBuffer)` as a parameter. The module-level `let logBuffer: string[] = []` at line 39 should be removed entirely.

---

**Important**

**`fetchImageCached` does not forward a non-OK image response before throwing**
Confidence: 82

At line 113-115, if the upstream image fetch returns a non-OK status, the function throws a generic `Error`. This is caught by the outer `catch` at line 246, which returns a `502` JSON error response regardless of the actual upstream status. If NASA's image CDN returns a `404` for a specific image URL (which can happen for older APODs), the caller gets a `502 { "error": "Image fetch failed: 404" }` rather than a more semantically correct response. More practically, the error message leaks the upstream status code into the client response with no way to distinguish a transient upstream failure from a configuration issue.

This is not a crash-level bug but it is a real gap — the `NasaApiError` class exists precisely to carry status codes, but `fetchImageCached` only throws plain `Error`. The upstream image status is lost by the time the outer catch runs. Consider throwing `new NasaApiError(res.status, ...)` here so the outer handler can map it correctly.

File: `/Users/grant/code/nasa-apod/src/index.ts`, lines 113-115

---

No issues found with the cache key scheme, the Cache API read/write mechanics, the `Cache-Control` header on stored responses, or the `clone()`/`put` pattern. Those are all correct.

   222→          "  Useful for wallpapers, <img> sources, and other zero-JS environments.",
   223→          "",
   224→          "  Usage:",
   225→          `    <img src="${origin}/">`,
   226→          "",
   227→          "  Routes:",
   228→          "    /        HD image (default)",
   229→          "    /sd      Standard resolution",
   230→          "    /info    JSON metadata",
   231→          "    /about   This page",
   232→          "",
   233→          "  Built by Grant Keller — https://grantkeller.dev",
   234→          "",
   235→        ].join("\n");
   236→
   237→        res = new Response(text, {
   238→          headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "text/plain; charset=utf-8" }),
   239→        });
   240→      } else {
   241→        res = new Response(
   242→          JSON.stringify({ error: "Not found. Routes: /, /sd, /info, /about" }),
   243→          { status: 404, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   244→        );
   245→      }
   246→    } catch (err) {
   247→      const message = err instanceof Error ? err.message : "Unknown error";
   248→      const status = err instanceof NasaApiError && err.statusCode === 429 ? 429 : 502;
   249→      res = new Response(
   250→        JSON.stringify({ error: message }),
   251→        { status, headers: baseHeaders(origin, { "Content-Type": "application/json" }) },
   252→      );
   253→    }
   254→
   255→    console.log(`────────────────────────────────────────`);
   256→    console.log(`${request.method} ${pathname} → ${res.status} (${Date.now() - start}ms)`);
   257→    for (const line of logBuffer) console.log(line);
   258→    logBuffer = [];
   259→    return res;
   260→  },
   261→};
   262→
```

_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 01:16:25Z)**_

Fixed. The reviewer found one real bug: the `logBuffer` was module-level, so concurrent requests would interleave each other's log lines. Now each request creates its own `log` array and threads it through `fetchApod`, `findLatestImage`, and `fetchImageCached` as a parameter. No shared mutable state.

---

+_**Agent (claude-opus-4-6 2026-03-27 01:05:13Z)**_
+
+
+
+The `[wrangler:info]` line is printed by wrangler after the response is sent — we can't control its ordering. It's wrangler's own request logging that fires after our handler returns, so it'll always come after any `console.log` calls we make during the handler.
